description_long: This is a bulbous, double-handled ceramic vase with a tall, narrow neck and a flared rim. The body is decorated with intricate geometric patterns, including triangles, zigzags, and spirals, rendered in faded black and reddish-brown pigments. The handles are small and rounded, positioned symmetrically on either side of the widest part of the vessel. One side of the vase is more heavily decorated, while the opposite side is mostly plain with visible wear and discoloration. The surface shows signs of age, with some areas of the original paint worn away and patches of reddish clay exposed. The overall form and ornamentation suggest it could be an artifact or a decorative piece suitable for display in a public or private urban setting.
description: A bulbous, double-handled ceramic vase with geometric patterns in black and reddish-brown, featuring a tall neck and flared rim. The surface shows signs of age and wear, with one side more decorated than the other.
description_view_0: Front view showing both handles and the most detailed geometric patterns on the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the transition from decorated to plain, with visible wear and a patch of exposed reddish clay.
description_view_2: Rear view with both handles visible, mostly plain surface with minimal decoration and more exposed clay.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the contrast between the decorated and plain sides, as well as the tall neck and flared rim.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museo Nacional de Historia Natural de Chile
